questions.question: Complete the table showing the business document and book of prime entry for transactions 2, 3 and 4.
questions.answer: Transaction 2: Credit note, Sales returns journal. Transaction 3: Paying-in slip, Cash book. Transaction 4: Bank statement, Cash book.

questions.question: Prepare the Petra account for the month of August 2020.
questions.answer: Petra account: Aug 1 Balance b/d $940, Aug 9 Sales $675, Aug 29 Bank $893, Aug 29 Discount allowed $47, Aug 31 Balance c/d $555. Total $1,615 each side. Sept 1 Balance b/d $555.

questions.question: State one current asset from the Petra account.
questions.answer: Current assets

questions.question: Explain one advantage and one disadvantage to Dev of offering a cash discount to credit customers.
questions.answer: Advantage: Increase profit for the year, Increase cash inflow. Disadvantage: May lose customers/sales may fall, Customers may take longer to pay.

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of Dev converting to a limited company and make a recommendation.
questions.answer: Advantages: Limited liability for debts, separate legal identity, access to different forms of finance. Disadvantages: Greater regulation, more complex accounting requirements, financial statements may be viewed by public. Recommendation: Should convert to limited company.

questions.question: Prepare the subscriptions account for BH Sports Club for the year ended 31 July 2020.
questions.answer: Subscriptions account: Aug 1 2019 Balance b/d $360, Jul 31 2020 Bank $9,960, Balance c/d $720, Jul 31 2020 Income and Expenditure $10,200, Balance c/d $120. Total $10,680 each side. Aug 1 2020 Balance b/d $720, Balance b/d $120.

questions.question: Prepare the trading account for BH Sports Club café for the year ended 31 July 2020.
questions.answer: Revenue $13,520, Opening inventory $520, Purchases $8,190 = $8,710, Less Closing inventory $770 = $7,940, Wages - café staff $2,770 = $10,710, Profit for the year $2,810.

questions.question: Prepare the income and expenditure account for BH Sports Club for the year ended 31 July 2020.
questions.answer: Income: Subscriptions $10,200, Profit on café $2,810 = $13,010. Expenditure: Wages - sports staff $5,220, Insurance - sports hall and sports equipment $1,390, Sports Hall expenses $3,360, Depreciation - sports hall $440, Depreciation - sports equipment $600 = $11,010. Surplus for the year $2,000.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by the accumulated fund of a club.
questions.answer: The difference between the assets and the liabilities, The accumulated surpluses earned since the club started.

questions.question: Calculate the accumulated fund of BH Sports Club at 31 July 2020.
questions.answer: Increase $2,000

questions.question: Calculate the cost of sales for JP Limited for the year ended 30 September 2020.
questions.answer: Opening inventory $66,000, Purchases $263,000, Carriage inwards $1,500 = $330,500, Less Closing inventory $59,000 = Cost of sales $271,500.

questions.question: Calculate the increase in the provision for doubtful debts for JP Limited for the year ended 30 September 2020.
questions.answer: Provision at 30 September 2020 ((80,500 - 500) x 2%) $1,600, Less Provision at 1 October 2019 $1,000 = Increase in provision $600.

questions.question: Prepare the income statement for JP Limited for the year ended 30 September 2020.
questions.answer: Revenue $529,500, Less Cost of sales $271,500 = Gross profit $258,000. Less expenses: Administrative expenses $42,800, Distribution costs $38,800, Irrecoverable debts $500, Provision for doubtful debts $600, Depreciation - non-current assets $61,000 = $143,700. Profit from operations $114,300, Debenture interest $1,400 = Profit for the year $112,900.

questions.question: Indicate the effect of each transaction on the capital employed of JP Limited.
questions.answer: Issue of additional debentures - Increase, Issue of additional ordinary shares - Increase, Payment of ordinary share dividend - Decrease, Proposal of ordinary share dividend - No effect, Transfer from retained earnings to general reserve - No effect.

questions.question: Calculate the profit for the year for 2020.
questions.answer: Profit for the year $16,000

questions.question: Calculate the profit margin, gross margin and return on capital employed (ROCE) for 2020.
Comment on the performance of the business using the ratios calculated in (b).
questions.answer: Profit margin: Has worsened from 8.56% to 6.67%. Possible causes: Increase in expenses, Poor control over expenses. Gross margin: Has worsened from 34.26% to 29.58%. Possible causes: Reduction in selling price, Purchasing from more expensive suppliers. ROCE: Has worsened from 32.08% to 25.81%. Possible causes: Increased capital employed, Less efficient use of resources.

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of introducing additional capital or taking a loan and make a recommendation.
questions.answer: Introduce additional capital: Advantages - Does not have to be repaid, No interest cost. Disadvantages - May not have enough available funds, Greater personal risk. Loan: Advantages - Instantly available, Has two years to pay it off. Disadvantages - Annual interest charged, Must be repaid, Security may be required. Recommendation: Should take a loan.

questions.question: Identify one interested party and explain their interest in the financial statements.
questions.answer: Suppliers/potential suppliers - To assess whether outstanding debts are likely to be paid. Bank - To assess the likelihood of loan/overdraft being repaid, To assess ability to pay interest, To assess availability of security. Lenders/potential lenders - To assess likelihood of loan repayment, To assess ability to pay interest, To assess security availability. Investors/potential partners - To assess future prospects, To assess profitability. Government/tax authorities - To assess tax due.

questions.question: Prepare the suspense account for Razia at 30 September 2020.
questions.answer: Suspense account: Sep 30 Sales returns $300, Sep 30 Difference on trial balance $200, Motor repairs $180, Rent receivable $310, Kamil $900, Rent payable $310. Total $1,100 each side.

questions.question: State the type of error for error 2 and error 4.
questions.answer: Error 2: Error of omission. Error 4: Error of transposition in one account.

questions.question: Indicate the effect of each error on the profit for the year.
questions.answer: Error 1: Decrease profit. Error 2: No effect on profit. Error 3: Decrease profit. Error 4: No effect on profit. Error 5: Increase profit. Error 6: No effect on profit. Error 7: Decrease profit.

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of selling only for cash and make a recommendation.
questions.answer: Advantages: Less book-keeping, No irrecoverable debts, Cash received immediately. Disadvantages: May lose customers/sales may reduce, May adversely affect customer relationships/customer loyalty, Increased security issues. Recommendation: Should continue selling on credit.
